prompt: A hand-drawn naturalist scientific illustration of a Seven-spot Ladybug (Coccinella septempunctata). a small rounded dome-shaped ladybird beetle viewed from above, glossy convex wing-cases a vivid scarlet to orange-red bearing exactly seven crisp round black spots (one shared spot at the top split across both wing-cases plus three on each side), a black head with small white cheek patches and tiny white-edged pronotum markings, six short black legs and short clubbed antennae tucked close beneath the compact oval body, classic rounded silhouette posed centered with generous even breathing room so the whole beetle and its legs sit well inside the circle. Vintage entomology field guide aesthetic with subtle ink and watercolor texture, warm earthy palette, single bug centered as hero on a plain warm cream/beige background (no scene, no environment), soft even lighting, gentle drop shadow beneath the bug, square 1:1 composition. CIRCULAR SAFE ZONE: the final image is cropped to a CIRCLE, so the ENTIRE insect — including every leg, antenna, wing, and tail streamer, plus its drop shadow — must sit fully inside the central inscribed circle with even breathing room on all sides. The bug occupies roughly 60-68 percent of the frame, comfortably centered; nothing touches or extends toward the edges or the four corners. Long appendages (antennae, legs, moth tails) are posed inward/curved so they stay within the circle rather than reaching the border. illustrated style not photorealistic, designed to be displayed as a circular thumbnail.
